_doc: Compiled from Compositional_metrics.txt + Diversity_signatures.txt — Framework v1.7

GMWI2.description: Overall health association score based on 155 taxa model (GMWI2). Positive = health-associated, negative = dysbiotic.

BR.description: Bias Ratio — fraction of health-association weight from supportive vs opportunistic taxa

CB.description: Count Balance — balance of supportive vs opportunistic species counts (not magnitudes)
CB.role: Supporting check on GMWI2/BR, not primary decision maker

wGMWI2.description: Abundance-weighted GMWI2 — health score adjusted by relative abundance (what IS vs what COULD BE)

SB.description: Signature Balance — log-ratio of supportive vs opportunistic abundances. Primary guild-dominance indicator.

HF.description: Health Fraction — scaled position between model's theoretical min and max. Empirically calibrated from reference microbiomes.

z_score.description: Deviation from healthy reference mean in SD units (SD ≈ 0.75)

pattern_classification.description: Five primary composite states from Framework PART A with stepwise algorithm

phase_1_presence.description: Establish baseline from GMWI2, BR, CB — what COULD BE the health state

mismatch_combinations.BR_green_GMWI2_red: Few strong negative taxa + many weak positive taxa → complex baseline
mismatch_combinations.BR_red_GMWI2_green: Few strong positives outweigh many weak negatives → fragile health potential
mismatch_combinations.CB_disagrees: Richness structure mismatched with coefficient weighting

phase_2_abundance.description: Reality check from wGMWI2, SB — what IS the ecological reality

comparisons.GMWI2_pos_wGMWI2_neg: Hidden dysbiosis: healthy taxa present but opportunists dominate biomass
comparisons.GMWI2_neg_wGMWI2_pos: Recovery pattern: dysbiotic diversity but health-associated dominants
comparisons.both_near_zero: Weak signal — rely on HF and z

phase_2_abundance.output: Dominance state + concordance flag + qualitative label

phase_3_reference.description: Context from HF, z — how typical or atypical is this

combinations.HF_depleted_z_negative: Well outside healthy envelope — clear depletion
combinations.HF_transitional_z_normal: Borderline on model extremes but population-typical

healthy_optimal.criteria: GMWI2 >= 0.5 AND wGMWI2 >= 0 AND SB >= 0.35 AND HF >= 0.62 AND |z| < 2
healthy_optimal.label: Healthy / Optimal
healthy_optimal.client_description: Your microbiome shows a strong, healthy composition with beneficial bacteria dominant at both the genetic and abundance levels.

transitional.criteria: Mixed green/orange across GMWI2, wGMWI2, SB, HF. No strong red flags.

subtypes.stable: Presence metrics green, abundance mildly orange — stable but room for improvement
subtypes.fragile: Presence mixed/orange, abundance clearly orange/red — at risk of sliding toward dysbiosis

transitional.label: Transitional
transitional.client_description: Your microbiome is in a transitional state — sitting between healthy and unhealthy, with potential for improvement in either direction.

presence_abundance_mismatch.criteria: GMWI2 and wGMWI2 have opposite signs (sign reversal)

subtypes.hidden_dysbiosis: GMWI2 >= 0.5 but wGMWI2 < 0 — good species present but not winning competition
subtypes.recovery_pattern: GMWI2 < 0 but wGMWI2 > 0 — health-associated dominants recovering

presence_abundance_mismatch.label: Presence-Abundance Mismatch
presence_abundance_mismatch.client_description_hidden: Your microbiome has good genetic diversity with many beneficial species present, but they're facing competitive pressure from other bacteria and not fully expressing their potential.
presence_abundance_mismatch.client_description_recovery: Your microbiome shows signs of recovery — health-promoting bacteria are gaining dominance even though the overall genetic profile still carries some legacy of past imbalance.

recovery_pattern.criteria: GMWI2 red/ambiguous BUT wGMWI2 > 0 AND SB > 0 AND HF >= transitional
recovery_pattern.label: Recovery Pattern
recovery_pattern.client_description: Your microbiome is actively recovering — health-promoting bacteria are gaining ground. The genetic profile still shows past imbalance, but the current dynamics are trending positively.

unhealthy_dysbiotic.criteria: GMWI2 <= -0.5 OR (wGMWI2 <= -0.10 AND SB < -0.35), AND HF <= 0.54 and/or z <= -1
unhealthy_dysbiotic.label: Unhealthy / Dysbiotic
unhealthy_dysbiotic.client_description: Your microbiome shows significant imbalance with opportunistic bacteria outcompeting beneficial ones, requiring targeted intervention.

summary_writing_structure.format: One-line headline (status + key driver) → Presence sentence → Abundance sentence → Reference sentence → Plain-language conclusion
summary_writing_structure.example: Transitional with Bifidobacteria Absence: Genetic presence shows borderline health (GMWI2 -0.19). Abundance weighting confirms concordant transitional state (wGMWI2 -0.004). Health Fraction places this in the middle tertile (0.557). Overall: ecosystem poised between states with intervention potential.

shannon.description: Shannon diversity index — combined species richness and evenness. Higher = more diverse ecosystem.
shannon.interpretation: Descriptive value. No explicit thresholds — interpretation driven mainly by Pielou evenness.

cross_rank_dysbiosis_check.description: Flags dysbiosis pattern when higher taxonomic rank has J>0.6 but deeper rank drops below 0.4
cross_rank_dysbiosis_check.rule: If family-level evenness > 0.6 AND species-level evenness < 0.4, flag hierarchical fragility
cross_rank_dysbiosis_check.interpretation: Apparent balance at family level masks severe dominance at species level — structural vulnerability hidden by taxonomy aggregation
cross_rank_dysbiosis_check.client_text: While your gut looks balanced at a broad level, zooming in reveals that within some bacterial families, one species dominates — this creates hidden fragility.

pielou_evenness.description: Species-level Pielou evenness J — how evenly distributed organisms are. Higher = more balanced.

green.label: Stable and resilient
green.headline: Community balance: Stable and resilient.
green.client_text: Species are well balanced rather than dominated by a few strains, which supports steady day-to-day function and recovery after stress.

amber.label: Imbalanced but coping
amber.headline: Community balance: Mildly imbalanced (watch zone).
amber.client_text: A few species hold more 'power' than others, so the system can work well in calm conditions but is easier to destabilize.

red.label: Dominated and fragile
red.headline: Community balance: Dominated and fragile.
red.client_text: A small group of microbes dominates, leaving little 'backup' if one group is disturbed, which increases the risk of symptoms when diet or stress changes.
